changes_summary: Addressed three significant flags: (1) FLAG-001 — clarified that scheduled direct posts match existing message-based behavior (temp files left for executor, not cleaned up), and that media_hints preserves original URLs for future re-download support; (2) FLAG-002 — added `user_details: {'direct_post': True}` to source_context metadata to satisfy XProvider's guard, since _build_tweet_caption uses provided tweet_text as-is when base_description is set; (3) FLAG-003 — changed direct mode entry condition from "has media_urls" to "no message_link and no message_id", which naturally supports text-only posts, direct retweets (action=retweet + target_post only), and media posts alike.
questions: For the publication record, is message_id=0 acceptable for the database schema (e.g. foreign key constraints, unique indexes on message_id+platform+action), or should we use a synthetic ID like a hash of the tweet_text?
Should route_key be required in direct-post mode (since there's no channel to resolve a route from), or is falling through to the guild's default route acceptable?

success_criteria.criterion: share_to_social accepts tweet_text (and optionally media_urls) without message_link/message_id and posts a tweet through the existing pipeline
Existing message-based share_to_social flow is completely unchanged — all current parameters and behaviors work identically
Direct-post mode supports scheduled_at, action (post/reply/retweet), target_post, and route_key parameters
Direct retweet (action=retweet + target_post, no message reference) works without tweet_text or media_urls
Direct post/reply returns error if tweet_text is missing
XProvider.publish() succeeds for direct posts — user_details stub passes the truthy guard at x_provider.py:31-33
Media files are cleaned up from temp directory after immediate publish (both success and failure)
Scheduled direct posts leave temp files for the executor, matching existing message-based scheduled behavior
All existing tests pass without modification
New code path adds fewer than 80 lines to execute_share_to_social
Tool schema description clearly documents direct-post mode and when to use media_urls vs message_link/message_id
Direct-post mode tested end-to-end with a real Supabase media URL posting to Twitter

assumptions: media_urls will be a JSON array of URL strings; we will not accept a single string (the LLM can wrap it in an array)
route_key is recommended but not required in direct-post mode — if omitted, route resolution uses guild_id + channel_id=0 which falls through to the guild's default route if one exists
message_id=0 and channel_id=0 are safe sentinel values for the publication record (no FK constraints in the social_publications table that would reject them)
user_id=0 is acceptable for direct posts since there is no Discord user associated with external media
The existing _download_media_from_url on sharer is reliable for Supabase URLs and does not need modification
Direct-post mode does not need duplicate detection since there is no stable message_id to deduplicate against

{'direct_post': True} as user_details satisfies the XProvider truthy guard and _build_tweet_caption uses the provided tweet_text as-is (line 111-112 of social_poster.py) without depending on user_details fields
Scheduled direct posts inherit the same temp-file persistence behavior as existing scheduled message-based posts — this is a known pre-existing limitation, not a new regression
When media_urls is provided alongside message_link/message_id, the message-based flow takes precedence and media_urls is ignored (no ambiguous dual-source behavior)
